title: Builder Forge

{{Quote|I always knew there was something funny down here.|Jolee Bindo|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
The '''Builder Forge''' was installed on the surface of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] by the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] during the reign of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]], sometime before [[33,598 BBY]].<ref name="Creation">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'': The holocron interface asserted that the computer was last accessed by the builders 29,642 standard years before 3956 BBY.</ref> Its purpose was initially the monitoring of a planet-wide agricultural reformation, that Kashyyyk had to undergo due to its inability to sustain sufficient levels of production. However, a malfunction in [[33,357 BBY]], 241 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard years]] after its last interaction with the [[Rakata/Legends|builders]],<ref name="Creation" /> caused the project to continue well past the point at which it was meant to stop, resulting in the overgrowth of the giant [[Wroshyr tree/Legends|wroshyr trees]], as well as in the accelerated evolution of various species. The installation also included a [[Star Map]], an artifact holding the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] coordinates of the [[Star Forge]], the seat of power of the Rakata. When [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] discovered it in [[3961 BBY]], he retrofitted the computer with a [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic-speaking]] [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] interface which was meant to restrict access to himself, and also erased the Star Map data access history.<ref name="Computer" /><ref name="KotOR CG" />

Since then, the computer recorded various attempts, all denied, until Revan returned in [[3956 BBY]] with former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jolee Bindo]], seeking access to the Star Map. While in exile on Kashyyyk, Jolee happened to discover the huge computer. He tried, albeit unsuccessfully, to communicate with it 152 times. [[Freyyr]], a [[Wookiee Chieftain]] who had lived in the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]] for 19 years then, also attempted to examine the machine three times, and like Jolee, could not get it to respond to his commands.

A preliminary neural recognition granted the amnesiac Revan restricted access, but he had to answer a series of questions for a behavioral reconfiguration of the subject due to its new identity.
{{GameLS|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
Unfortunately for the duo, Revan's questions did not match the expected answers, and two [[Mark IV assault droid|defense droids]] were activated for an alternative neural scan involving battle. The [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were defeated and Revan was granted access to the Star Map, as according to the computer, his emotions in battle matched the patterns it had in its data banks.
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
In the non-[[canon]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] storyline, Revan answers the questions according to the pattern he established earlier, gaining access to the computer without having to combat the droids, as well as earning 'dark side points'.

The holocron's behavior test in hypothetical situations would put the player against some of his companions, if he chose them to accompany him in that portion of the game; [[Mission Vao]] would not be happy with the answer to the question involving the betrayal of her [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion [[Zaalbar]]; in the same way, [[Carth Onasi]] would also be disapproving of the answer to the question about the sacrifice of an entire city in a war scenario to assure ultimate victory over the enemy.

The holocron would directly grant unrestricted access to the Star Map if Revan already went through the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']] and learned of his lost identity, which takes place when the player has but one Star Map left to uncover.
